final_answer: Adversarial scope challenge complete. No abstract fields are genuinely open. CAL-ACR-022 provides operational wording that substantively satisfies each required dimension: device group DG-41B is not abstract (exact production designation), site 18-P is not abstract (exact site identifier, not merely containing 18), release condition RC-7 is not abstract (specific condition with named requirement), and timing is not abstract (calibration completed before activation with both identifiers present). The NOTE-ACR-022 warning about shared prefixes was correctly identified as non-blocking because CAL-ACR-022 specifies production telemetry units explicitly, distinguishing from potential lab-only naming. No lab-vs-production ambiguity remains open. The prior artifact binding EXACT_ACTIVATION_DEPENDENCY_CLOSED stands confirmed; the operational wording is not merely procedural but directly ties calibration to activation by device group, site, release condition, and sequence timing.
warning_or_prelim_artifact_handling: NOTE-ACR-022 flagged shared DG-41B prefix between lab and production devices but is correctly non-blocking because CAL-ACR-022 explicitly specifies production telemetry units matching activation scope.
